---
title: "Atlantic Silverside Microbiome Analysis Workflow with Statistical Analysis"
author: "Clara C. Tucker"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
---

# Introduction

Increasing atmospheric CO2 levels intensify ocean warming and acidification, impacting marine organism fitness. This project examines the combined effects of these stressors on Atlantic silverside (*Menidia menidia*) holobionts.  

**Experimental design**:

- Preliminary 2x2 factorial: temperatures 17°C, 24°C; pH 7.2, 8.2; fish ages 0 dph, 10 dph; 3 biological replicates per treatment  
- Ongoing 3x3 factorial: temperatures 17°C, 20°C, 24°C; pH 7.2, 7.5, 8.2; fish 10 dph  

This pipeline integrates **QIIME1, QIIME2, DADA2**, and downstream **statistical analyses**.

---

# 1. Data Preparation and FASTQ Conversion (QIIME1)

```bash
# Convert FASTA + QUAL to FASTQ
convert_fastaqual_fastq.py -f input.fasta -q input.qual -o output_dir -c fasta_qual_to_fastq -F -b

# Check number of lines
wc -l output_dir/input.fastq

# Split large FASTQ files into manageable parts
# Use fastq-splitter: http://kirill-kryukov.com/study/tools/fastq-splitter/

# 2. Barcode Extraction and Validation
# Extract barcodes (Mr. DNA data)
extract_barcodes.py -f input.fastq -o output_dir -c barcode_paired_stitched -l --bc1_len 8 --bc2_len 8

# Validate mapping file
check_id_map.py -m mapping_file.txt -o output_dir

# 3. Demultiplexing and Quality Filtering (QIIME1)
# Split libraries and assign barcodes
split_libraries_fastq.py -i split_fastq_part.fastq -b barcodes.fastq -m mapping_file.txt --barcode_type hamming_8 -o output_dir

# Validate demultiplexed sequences
validate_demultiplexed_fasta.py -m mapping_file.txt -i seqs.fna -o output_dir

# 4. OTU Picking (QIIME1)
# Closed-reference OTU picking
pick_closed_reference_otus.py -i seqs.fna -o otu_output_dir
#Export OTU tables for Excel-based contaminant filtering.

# 5. Import to QIIME2
qiime tools import \
  --type EMPSingleEndSequences \
  --input-path Exp1_directory \
  --output-path Exp1_emp-single-end-sequences.qza

qiime tools import \
  --type EMPSingleEndSequences \
  --input-path Exp23_directory \
  --output-path Exp23_emp-single-end-sequences.qza

# 6. Demultiplexing and Primer Removal (QIIME2)
# Demultiplex
qiime demux emp-single \
  --i-seqs Exp1_emp-single-end-sequences.qza \
  --m-barcodes-file Exp1_mapping.txt \
  --m-barcodes-category BarcodeSequence \
  --o-per-sample-sequences Exp1_demux.qza

qiime demux emp-single \
  --i-seqs Exp23_emp-single-end-sequences.qza \
  --m-barcodes-file Mapping_2.txt \
  --m-barcodes-category BarcodeSequence \
  --o-per-sample-sequences Exp23_demux.qza

# Primer trimming
qiime cutadapt trim-single \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences Exp1_demux.qza \
  --p-front GTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTA \
  --p-error-rate 0 \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trimmed_Exp1.qza

qiime cutadapt trim-single \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences Exp23_demux.qza \
  --p-front GTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTA \
  --p-error-rate 0 \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trimmed_Exp23.qza

# 7. DADA2 Pipeline in R
library(dada2)
library(phyloseq)

# Paths to trimmed FASTQ
trimmed_path1 <- "trimmed_Exp1"
trimmed_path23 <- "trimmed_Exp23"

fastqFs1 <- list.files(trimmed_path1, pattern="fastq", full.names=TRUE)
fastqFs23 <- list.files(trimmed_path23, pattern="fastq", full.names=TRUE)

filtpathF1 <- file.path(trimmed_path1, "filtered")
filtpathF23 <- file.path(trimmed_path23, "filtered")

filterAndTrim(fwd=fastqFs1, filt=file.path(filtpathF1, basename(fastqFs1)), truncLen=250, trimLeft=30)
filterAndTrim(fwd=fastqFs23, filt=file.path(filtpathF23, basename(fastqFs23)), truncLen=250, trimLeft=30)

# Dereplication
derepFs1 <- derepFastq(list.files(filtpathF1, full.names=TRUE))
derepFs23 <- derepFastq(list.files(filtpathF23, full.names=TRUE))

# Learn error rates
errFs1 <- learnErrors(list.files(filtpathF1, full.names=TRUE))
errFs23 <- learnErrors(list.files(filtpathF23, full.names=TRUE))

# Sample inference
dadaFs1 <- dada(derepFs1, err=errFs1)
dadaFs23 <- dada(derepFs23, err=errFs23)

# OTU tables
seqtab1 <- makeSequenceTable(dadaFs1)
seqtab23 <- makeSequenceTable(dadaFs23)
seqtab_combined <- mergeSequenceTables(seqtab1, seqtab23)

# 8. Alpha Diversity Analysis
# QIIME1
compare_alpha_diversity.py -i PD_whole_tree.txt -m mapping.txt -c Treatment -d 100 -o Treatment_PD100

# R Two-way ANOVA
library(tidyverse)

alpha_data <- read.csv("PD_whole_tree.txt", sep="\t")
anova_result <- aov(Shannon ~ Temperature * pH, data=alpha_data)
summary(anova_result)
TukeyHSD(anova_result)

# 9. Beta Diversity Analysis
# QIIME1
# Compute mean/median distance matrices
dissimilarity_mtx_stats.py -i dists/ -o dist_stats/

# PERMANOVA
compare_categories.py --method adonis -i unweighted_unifrac_dm.txt -m Fasting_Map.txt -c Treatment -o permanova_out -n 10000

# Boxplots
make_distance_boxplots.py -d unweighted_unifrac_dm.txt -m Fasting_Map.txt -f "Treatment" -o out1

# Principal Coordinates & 2D plots
principal_coordinates.py -i beta_div.txt -o beta_div_coords.txt
make_2d_plots.py -i unweighted_unifrac_pc.txt -m Fasting_Map.txt

# 10. Differential Abundance Analysis
differential_abundance.py -i otu_table.biom -o diff_otus.txt -m map.txt -a DESeq2_nbinom -c Treatment -x Control -y Fast -d

# 11. OTU Taxonomy Annotation
for i in $(ls directory_name); do
    assign_taxonomy.py -i directory_name/$i -m blast -o output_directory/$i
done
# 12. Summary
Age strongly predicts microbiome diversity (alpha & beta)

Temperature increases species richness; pH has minimal effect alone

Temperature x pH interaction affects diversity at 10 dph

Differential abundance identifies OTUs responding to environmental stressors

Results provide insight into holobiont adaptation potential under climate change

# 13. References

https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/tutorial.html
http://qiime.org/scripts/
https://docs.qiime2.org/2023.8/tutorials/moving-pictures/
https://github.com/qiime2/q2-cutadapt
https://www.r-bloggers.com/r-tutorial-series-two-way-anova-with-pairwise-comparisons/
